title: DO-326A Plan for Security Aspects of Certification (PSecAA) register and milestone tracker for type certifications and major modifications, with FAA / EASA finding-of-non-compliance early-warning
exclusions: Excludes minor changes that the FAA Aircraft Certification Office (ACO) or the EASA project certification manager (PCM) has determined do not require cybersecurity findings (e.g. cosmetic interior changes, paint schemes). Excludes Part-23 small-aeroplane projects below the cyber threshold defined in the project's PSecAA scope justification.
evidence: Saved search `UC-22.60.1` archived daily to `audit_evidence` with `do326a_clause="DO-326A-s2-2"`. Every row pins (project_id, applicant, type_certificate_or_stc, airframe_type, psecaa_version, psecaa_acceptance_status, certification_authority, milestone_due_date, milestone_status, days_to_due, risk_score).

compliance.assurance_rationale: DO-326A §2.2 requires every airworthiness security project to maintain a Plan for Security Aspects of Certification (PSecAA) that the certification authority reviews and accepts early in the project. This UC implements the fleet-wide PSecAA register and surfaces milestone slippage that would expose the project to a finding of non-compliance.
compliance.controlObjective: Demonstrates that every active TC/STC project has a current, accepted PSecAA and all DO-326A milestones (PSecAA acceptance, CSI baseline, SRA, security architecture freeze, verification, CASI delivery) are on track.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search archived to `audit_evidence`, the `DO-326A PSecAA Milestone Health` dashboard, the certification-authority acceptance letters, the configuration-managed PSecAA document versions.

compliance.assurance_rationale: FAA AC 20-186 §5 accepts DO-326A as the means of compliance for FAA Aircraft Network Security Program demonstration; the PSecAA is the foundational document that FAA AIR reviews.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidences that FAA-bound projects have a PSecAA acceptable to the FAA Aircraft Certification Service.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Same as the primary clause, plus the FAA ACO acceptance letter for each PSecAA.

compliance.assurance_rationale: EASA AMC 20-42 §4 accepts ED-202A as the means of compliance for EASA airworthiness-security findings; the PSecAA equivalent is reviewed by the EASA project certification manager.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidences that EASA-bound projects have a PSecAA / Security Plan acceptable to the EASA project certification manager.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Same as the primary clause, plus the EASA PCM acceptance email/letter for each PSecAA.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Allow a project's PSecAA acceptance milestone to slip past its due date without an updated PSecAA submission. UC-22.60.1 MUST flag within 24 hours with risk_score >= 80.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Submit and obtain certification-authority PSecAA acceptance 30 days before the milestone. UC-22.60.1 MUST record `milestone_status=accepted` and `risk_score=0`.

dataSources: KV Store `do326a_psecaa_lookup` (project_id, applicant, type_certificate_or_stc, airframe_type, psecaa_version, psecaa_acceptance_status, certification_authority, milestone_id, milestone_due_date, milestone_completed_at, authority_acceptance_letter_ref). ServiceNow GRC project register (`snow:risk`).
app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (Splunkbase 1621).
spl:
| inputlookup do326a_psecaa_lookup
| eval now_ts = now()
| eval days_to_due = round((milestone_due_date - now_ts) / 86400, 1)
| eval days_overdue = case(isnull(milestone_completed_at) AND now_ts > milestone_due_date, round((now_ts - milestone_due_date) / 86400, 1), 1=1, 0)
| eval milestone_status = case(
    isnotnull(milestone_completed_at), "complete",
    isnotnull(authority_acceptance_letter_ref), "accepted",
    isnull(milestone_completed_at) AND now_ts > milestone_due_date, "overdue",
    days_to_due <= 14, "at-risk",
    1=1, "in-window")
| eval risk_score = case(
    milestone_status = "overdue" AND days_overdue > 30, 100,
    milestone_status = "overdue" AND days_overdue > 7, 90,
    milestone_status = "overdue", 80,
    milestone_status = "at-risk" AND psecaa_acceptance_status != "accepted", 70,
    milestone_status = "at-risk", 40,
    1=1, 0)
| where risk_score > 0
| sort 0 -risk_score, -days_overdue, +days_to_due
| table project_id applicant type_certificate_or_stc airframe_type psecaa_version psecaa_acceptance_status certification_authority milestone_id milestone_due_date days_to_due days_overdue milestone_status risk_score
description: Reconciles every active type-certification (TC) and major-modification (STC) project against its DO-326A PSecAA acceptance, then tracks all downstream milestones (CSI register baseline, SRA completion, security architecture freeze, security verification, CASI delivery) against their certification-authority-agreed due dates. Surfaces overdue and at-risk milestones to the Chief Engineer and the CISO before they become a formal finding of non-compliance.
value: A finding of non-compliance with the PSecAA at FAA or EASA finalisation can delay type-certificate (TC) issuance by 3-12 months, with knock-on revenue loss measured in hundreds of millions of dollars per programme. The Boeing 737 MAX cyber recertification and the Airbus A350 ANSP project both demonstrated that DO-326A artefacts are on the critical path for first-flight and entry-into-service. This UC closes the gap before the certification authority sees it.
implementation: (1) Populate `do326a_psecaa_lookup` from the engineering programme-management system (Siemens Teamcenter, PTC Windchill, Dassault ENOVIA) and from the certification team's GRC tool (ServiceNow GRC, RSA Archer). (2) The Chief Engineer or designated security focal updates the milestone_completed_at and authority_acceptance_letter_ref fields when the FAA ACO or EASA PCM issues acceptance. (3) Schedule UC-22.60.1 daily at 06:00 programme-local; route `risk_score >= 80` to the Chief Engineer + CISO + Programme Director at severity P1, `risk_score >= 60` at severity P2. (4) Build the `DO-326A PSecAA Milestone Health` dashboard with per-programme, per-authority, and per-airframe-type views.
visualization: (Panel 1) Active projects by PSecAA acceptance status (accepted / submitted / draft / overdue) - stacked bar by certification authority. (Panel 2) Milestone heat map (project x days-to-due). (Panel 3) Open critical-risk projects sorted by risk_score. (Panel 4) 12-month milestone-on-time performance trend.

references.title: RTCA DO-326A - Airworthiness Security Process Specification
FAA AC 20-186 - Airworthiness and Operational Approval for Aircraft Network Security Program (ANSP)
EASA AMC 20-42 - Airworthiness Information Security Risk Assessment

knownFalsePositives:
1. **Authority-approved milestone deferral**: the FAA ACO / EASA PCM may agree to a milestone deferral (typically via a written re-baseline of the project schedule); ensure the new due date is loaded into the KV Store with the authority concurrence reference. 2. **Programme on hold**: a project that is paused pending business decision (programme parking) should be marked with `project_status=on-hold` and excluded from the alert. 3. **PSecAA Revision in flight**: a PSecAA Revision being prepared in response to scope change is briefly in `draft` status; allow a 30-day grace from the trigger event.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `do326a_22_60_1_exceptions` with the Chief Engineer + CISO documented approval, an explicit expiry date, and a written justification.
reviewer: Aviation Cybersecurity SME Panel + Chief Engineer + Programme Director + CISO
detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

- Splunk Enterprise >= 9.2 or Splunk Cloud.
- KV Store `do326a_psecaa_lookup` (created via `collections.conf` and seeded from the programme-management system).
- ServiceNow GRC project register or equivalent.
- A documented mapping from internal milestone IDs to DO-326A artefact deliverables.

## Step 1 - PSecAA KV Store

```ini
[do326a_psecaa_lookup]
enforceTypes = true
field.project_id = string
field.applicant = string
field.type_certificate_or_stc = string
field.airframe_type = string
field.psecaa_version = string
field.psecaa_acceptance_status = string
field.certification_authority = string
field.milestone_id = string
field.milestone_due_date = time
field.milestone_completed_at = time
field.authority_acceptance_letter_ref = string
field.project_status = string
```

Seed from the programme-management system on a daily schedule (modular input or `outputlookup`).

## Step 2 - Milestone catalogue

Maintain a milestone catalogue mapping internal IDs to DO-326A artefacts: PSecAA acceptance, CSI register baseline, Threat Conditions list freeze, Security Risk Assessment completion, Security Architecture freeze, Security Effectiveness Demonstration, CASI delivery to operator.

## Step 3 - Schedule

```ini
[UC-22.60.1: DO-326A PSecAA Milestone Health]
cron_schedule = 0 6 * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -1d@d
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index, notable
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.60.1,reg=DO-326A,clause=DO-326A-s2-2
alert.severity = 5
```

## Step 4 - Runbooks

**`risk_score >= 80`** (owner: Chief Engineer + CISO, severity P1): Within 24 hours, schedule a programme-status call with the FAA ACO / EASA PCM; within 7 days, deliver a written remediation plan with an acceptable new milestone date.

**`risk_score 40-79`** (owner: Security Focal, severity P2): Within 5 business days, drive milestone closure.

## Step 5 - Aviation-specific notes

- **Joint TC validation**: a project may have parallel FAA / EASA / TCCA / CAA review; ensure each authority's milestone clock is tracked independently.
- **Major modification vs Type Certificate**: STCs have a lighter PSecAA but still require acceptance; do not exclude them from monitoring.
- **Issue Papers / CRIs**: legacy projects may have project-specific Special Conditions (Issue Papers in the US, Certification Review Items in EASA); include their milestones in the catalogue.
grandmaExplanation: Before a new aircraft can fly with passengers, the regulator must sign off on its cyber-security plan. We watch every project that is going through this approval and make sure each step is on track, so the aircraft isn't delayed.
